message:
Add UI data layer for conversation view materialization

This commit introduces a dedicated data access layer for the TUI that
decouples view rendering from direct storage operations, enabling efficient
conversation detail loading with fully hydrated message lists.

New module: src/ui/data.rs

Core types:
- ConversationView: aggregates Conversation + Vec<Message> + Option<Workspace>
  into a single view model optimized for display in the TUI detail pane

Key functions:
- load_conversation(db_path, source_path): materializes a complete
  conversation view by source_path with a single SQL query
- load_messages(db_path, conversation_id): fetches all messages for a
  conversation ordered by creation timestamp
- Both functions return Result<Option<T>> for graceful handling of
  missing/invalid data

Query optimization:
- load_conversation performs a LEFT JOIN across conversations, agents,
  workspaces to minimize round-trips
- Filters by source_path (unique identifier from connector ingestion)
- Orders by started_at DESC to prefer most recent when duplicates exist
- Single LIMIT 1 for fast query plan (indexed source_path column)

Design rationale:
- Separates view concerns from storage schema (ui/ doesn't know SQLite details)
- Enables future refactoring to different storage backends without TUI changes
- Provides type-safe materialization with proper None handling
- Reuses existing domain types (Conversation, Message, Workspace) from model/

Integration points:
- TUI detail pane: calls load_conversation when user selects a conversation
- Message browser: uses load_messages for paginated message display
- Preview rendering: ConversationView provides all needed data in one struct

This layer will be consumed by the TUI improvements in the next commit,
which add interactive conversation browsing and message detail views.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
